industries.name: Rare Earth Mining & Separation
industries.share: 100% of Nd must pass through this supply chain
uses: Mining of bastnäsite, monazite, and ion-adsorption clay ores containing neodymium
Solvent extraction and ion-exchange separation to isolate NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) oxide from mixed REE (rare earth elements) streams
Metal reduction to produce neodymium metal (electrolysis of NdF₃ (neodymium fluoride))
Alloying with iron and boron to produce NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) alloy flakes for magnet manufacturing

companies.name: China Rare Earth Group
companies.use: State-owned group controlling the majority of China's rare earth mining and separation. Produces the bulk of the world's NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) oxide through operations in Inner Mongolia and Jiangxi.

companies.name: Lynas Rare Earths
companies.use: World's largest non-Chinese rare earth producer. Mines at Mt Weld, Western Australia (world's richest REE (rare earth elements) deposit) and processes at LAMP facility in Malaysia.

companies.name: MP Materials
companies.use: Operates Mountain Pass mine in California — the only producing rare earth mine in the USA. Completed NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) separation facility in 2022; supplies magnet-grade material to General Motors.

companies.name: Neo Performance Materials
companies.use: Processes separated rare earth materials at facilities in Estonia and China. Supplies NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) oxide and magnet alloys to European and North American magnet manufacturers.

companies.name: Energy Fuels
companies.use: Began producing commercial NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) oxide from its White Mesa Mill in Utah in 2023. A key element of US government efforts to rebuild domestic rare earth processing capacity.

companies.name: Arafura Resources
companies.use: Developing the Nolan's NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) project in Australia's Northern Territory. Holds one of the world's highest-grade NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) deposits outside China; targeted to begin production ~2027.

industries.name: NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) Magnet Manufacturing
uses: Sintered NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) magnets (highest energy density of any permanent magnet) for motors and generators
Bonded NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for compact, complex-shaped components in sensors and consumer electronics
Hot-deformed NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for textured ring magnets in electric power steering and robotics
Dysprosium-enhanced NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for high-temperature EV motor applications (>150°C)
Grain-boundary diffusion treatment to reduce heavy REE (rare earth elements) content while maintaining performance

companies.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical
companies.use: World's largest NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) producer outside China. Major supplier of high-performance sintered magnets for hard disk drives, wind turbines, and increasingly EV traction motors.

companies.name: Beijing Zhong Ke San Huan (ZKSH)
companies.use: China's largest NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) magnet producer. Supplies magnets for consumer electronics, EV motors, and industrial applications. Key link in China's fully integrated REE (rare earth elements) supply chain.

companies.name: TDK Corporation
companies.use: Major Japanese NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) producer with factories in Japan, China, and Germany. Primary supplier of hard disk drive voice coil and spindle magnets; expanding into EV motor magnets.

companies.name: Proterial (formerly Hitachi Metals)
companies.use: Holds foundational NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) patents (Sagawa patents). Key supplier of high-performance sintered magnets to Japanese automakers for hybrid and EV traction motors.

companies.name: Vacuumschmelze (VAC)
companies.use: Europe's leading rare earth magnet producer. Supplies NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) magnets for wind turbines, industrial motors, and medical devices. A key element of European magnet sovereignty efforts.

companies.name: Arnold Magnetic Technologies
companies.use: USA's primary manufacturer of NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) and Sm-Co (samarium-cobalt) permanent magnets. Key supplier to US defense contractors and industrial motor manufacturers. Working on domestic magnet sourcing.

industries.name: Electric Vehicle Propulsion
industries.share: ~20% of Nd consumption and rapidly growing
uses: Interior permanent magnet (IPM (interior permanent magnet)) synchronous motors consuming ~1.5–3 kg NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) per vehicle
Axial flux motors using concentrated NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for higher power density in performance EVs
E-axle drive units integrating NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) motor, reducer, and inverter for space efficiency
NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in wheel-hub direct-drive motors for commercial EVs and autonomous vehicles
Traction motor magnets must meet IEC 60034 for sustained high-temperature performance

companies.name: BYD
companies.use: World's largest EV and plug-in hybrid manufacturer (3M+ vehicles/year). Vertically integrated: owns magnet suppliers and is investing in NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) processing. Largest single EV consumer of Nd.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.use: Uses IPM (interior permanent magnet) motors with NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in Model 3, Model Y, and Cybertruck. Model 3 rear motor uses ~2 kg NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron). Produced 1.8M vehicles in 2023. Sources magnets from multiple Asian suppliers.

companies.name: Toyota Motor
companies.use: World's largest automaker; pioneered NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) use in the Prius hybrid motor since 1997. Produces 3+ million hybrids annually, each using ~1–2 kg NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron). Has invested in Lynas for supply security.

companies.name: Volkswagen Group
companies.use: Rapidly scaling EV production (ID.3, ID.4, Audi e-tron). Signed supply agreements with rare earth companies to secure Nd supply for German and European manufacturing plants.

companies.name: General Motors
companies.use: Uses NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) motors in Chevrolet Bolt, Silverado EV, and GMC Hummer EV. Signed direct supply agreement with MP Materials for US-sourced magnets beginning 2025.

companies.name: BMW Group
companies.use: NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) motors in BMW i4, iX, and Mini Electric. Developing next-generation circular economy magnet recycling programs to reclaim Nd from end-of-life EV motors.

industries.name: Wind Energy Generation
uses: Direct-drive permanent magnet generators (PMG (permanent magnet generator)s) using 300–600 kg NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) per MW of capacity
Geared permanent magnet generators (hybrid drivetrains) for offshore wind applications
NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) pole pieces in doubly-fed induction generators for onshore wind
High-temperature-grade NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) required for generator windings operating at 80–120°C
Recyclability of NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) magnets from decommissioned turbines is a growing industry focus

companies.name: Goldwind
companies.share: ~18% of PMG (permanent magnet generator) turbine market
companies.use: World's second-largest wind turbine maker and pioneer of direct-drive PMG (permanent magnet generator) technology. Its 3–6 MW turbines each contain ~500–900 kg of NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) magnets. Largest PMG (permanent magnet generator) turbine fleet globally.

companies.name: Vestas
companies.share: ~14% of PMG (permanent magnet generator) turbine market
companies.use: World's largest wind turbine manufacturer. The V236-15.0 MW offshore turbine uses direct-drive PMG (permanent magnet generator) with ~3+ tonnes of NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) magnets. Commissioned RE supply chain study for circularity.

companies.name: Siemens Gamesa
companies.share: ~12% of PMG (permanent magnet generator) turbine market
companies.use: Major offshore wind turbine supplier using direct-drive PMG (permanent magnet generator) in the SG 14–236 DD. Strong presence in European offshore wind; requires high-temperature NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for North Sea conditions.

companies.name: GE Vernova
companies.use: Makes the Haliade-X (14–15 MW) offshore wind turbine with permanent magnet generator. Partnered with MP Materials to explore US-sourced NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for American wind turbine manufacturing.

companies.name: Enercon
companies.use: German turbine pioneer using direct-drive annular generator technology. Primarily onshore market; its E-160 and E-175 turbines use NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for high-torque, low-speed direct-drive generation.

companies.name: Ming Yang Smart Energy
companies.use: Rapidly growing Chinese OEM deploying large direct-drive offshore turbines (MySE 16.0–242). Uses high-performance NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in generators for China's offshore wind expansion program.

industries.name: Consumer Electronics & Hard Drives
uses: Voice coil motors (VCM — voice coil motor) in hard disk drive read/write heads — ~5 g NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) per HDD
Spindle drive magnets holding the rotating disk platters at precise speed
NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in smartphone vibration motors, loudspeakers, and camera optical image stabilization
Earbuds and headphone drivers using NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for high-sensitivity sound reproduction
NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in precision actuators for laptops, robotics, and industrial automation

companies.name: Seagate Technology
companies.use: World's largest hard disk drive maker. Each HDD uses ~5g of NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in the voice coil motor and spindle. Produces ~170M+ units/year. Studying NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) recycling from decommissioned drives.

companies.name: Western Digital
companies.use: Major HDD producer (HGST + WD brands). Significant NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) consumer for data center storage drives. Also acquires NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) for flash storage device actuators.

companies.name: Apple
companies.use: NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in iPhone Taptic Engine (vibration), MagSafe charger array, MacBook speakers, AirPods drivers, and Apple Watch. Apple sources REE (rare earth elements) supply chain data from suppliers under its conflict minerals policy.

companies.name: Samsung Electronics
companies.use: NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in Galaxy smartphone haptic motors, Galaxy Buds speakers, and QLED TV speaker systems. Samsung is investing in rare earth supply chain transparency under its ESG strategy.

companies.name: Sony Group
companies.use: NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) in PlayStation DualSense controller haptic actuators, Sony WH-1000XM headphone drivers, and digital cinema projector lens actuators.

companies.name: Nidec Corporation
companies.use: World's largest producer of small precision motors. Uses NdFeB (neodymium-iron-boron) extensively in HDD spindle motors, fan motors, and automotive actuators. Supplies to virtually all major electronics OEMs.
